meta.title: 4 Days Lombok's Best-Kept Secret | From RM988 | Dhesu Travel
meta.description: Discover Lombok's hidden treasures in 4 days — Sendang Gile Waterfall, Gili Trawangan, Benang Stokel & Benang Kelambu waterfalls, Sasak villages. Private tour from RM988. Book with Dhesu Travel.
meta.keywords: Lombok 4 days tour, Lombok waterfall tour, Sendang Gile waterfall, Benang Stokel, Gili Trawangan snorkelling, Lombok nature tour Malaysia, Indonesia holiday package

hero.title: 4 Days Lombok
hero.intro: For nature lovers and soul seekers: exquisite beaches, world-class surf breaks, volcanic peaks, mesmerising waterfalls, and vibrant coral reefs — all in one breathtaking island. This 4-day private tour reveals Lombok's hidden side that even many Indonesians haven't seen.
hero.heroImageAlt: Lush jungle waterfall in Lombok's Rinjani foothills

includes: All meals as specified (Day 1 & 2 & 3: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner; Day 4: Breakfast)

overview.heading: Lombok Beyond the Postcard — Waterfalls, Volcanoes & Island Life
body: Most visitors see Lombok's famous beaches and Gili Islands — this itinerary goes further. Over four days, your private guide takes you into Rinjani's rainforest foothills to discover cascading waterfalls hidden among ancient banyan trees, into traditional Sasak villages where customs unchanged since the 15th century are still alive, and out to Gili Trawangan for world-class snorkelling over coral gardens.

This package is designed for travellers who want more than a beach holiday — you'll hike to twin waterfalls, learn Sasak weaving traditions, explore a sacred old mosque, and watch the sun set from Merese Hill. With all meals, transport, entrance fees, and your dedicated guide included, there's nothing to organise — just show up and explore.

items.title: Sendang Gile Waterfall
items.body: A short jungle hike from Senaru Village at the base of Mount Rinjani leads to this spectacular 31-metre waterfall. The spray creates a natural mist that cools the air as you stand at the base — one of Lombok's most rewarding nature experiences.
items.imageAlt: Sendang Gile waterfall cascading through lush Rinjani rainforest

items.title: Benang Stokel & Benang Kelambu Waterfalls
items.body: Lombok's twin waterfall gems: Benang Stokel drops in two tiers through forest while Benang Kelambu's curtain-like streams flow through hanging green vegetation — one of the most unique waterfall formations in Indonesia.
items.imageAlt: Twin waterfalls Benang Stokel and Benang Kelambu in Lombok jungle

items.title: Gili Trawangan Island
items.body: A 15-minute boat ride to Lombok's most beloved car-free island — powdery white sand, turquoise water, and spectacular coral reef snorkelling. The laid-back island vibe and stunning sunsets make it a must on any Lombok itinerary.
items.imageAlt: Turquoise water and white sand at Gili Trawangan island

items.title: Sasak Cultural Immersion
items.body: From the 15-generation Sade Village to the ancient Bayan Mosque and the intricate gold-thread Songket weavers of Sukarara — this itinerary immerses you in authentic Sasak culture in ways no resort holiday can replicate.
items.imageAlt: Sasak women weaving traditional Songket fabric in Sukarara village

days.title: Sasak Tribe > Kuta Beach > Merese Hill > Banyumulek Pottery Village
days.description: Your guide meets you at Lombok International Airport and the adventure begins. After a welcome lunch at a local restaurant, discover the living culture of Sade Village — home to the Sasak tribe for over 15 generations, where traditional thatched-roof homes and ancient customs remain unchanged. Head to Kuta Beach, famous for its world-class surf breaks and dramatic coastal scenery, then stop at the Mandalika International Street Circuit — Indonesia's newest MotoGP venue. As the afternoon turns golden, climb Merese Hill for sweeping panoramic views over Kuta Bay and Tanjung Aan Beach. Visit Sukarara Village, where women weave intricate Songket fabric with gold and silver threads using handlooms, and browse the work of the skilled potters at Banyumulek Pottery Village. A sunset dinner at Senggigi Beach concludes a rich first day on the island.
suggestedActivities: Explore traditional Sasak homes at Sade Village
Walk the surf beach at Kuta Lombok
Photograph the panoramic view from Merese Hill
Watch Songket weaving demonstrations at Sukarara

days.title: Malimbu Hill > Gili Trawangan > Sendang Gile Waterfall > Pusuk Pass
days.description: After breakfast, drive north through Lombok's dramatic coastal road, stopping at Malimbu Beach for sweeping panoramic hilltop views over the Gili Islands. Take the 15-minute boat transfer to Gili Trawangan for the morning — snorkel over vibrant coral reefs, swim in crystal-clear turquoise water, and enjoy the car-free island atmosphere. After lunch, head to Senaru Village at the base of Mount Rinjani for a short walk through tropical forest to reach the spectacular 31-metre Sendang Gile Waterfall, one of Lombok's most rewarding natural attractions. Visit the ancient Old Mosque at Bayan Village, one of the oldest Islamic sites on the island. Drive back through Pusuk Pass, a protected forest road where playful long-tailed macaques line the roadside, stopping to browse Sasak wood carvings and Cukli shell-inlay crafts at the Sasela art market. Dinner at a local restaurant and overnight in Lombok.
suggestedActivities: Snorkelling at Gili Trawangan coral gardens
Visit the ancient Old Mosque at Bayan

days.title: Benang Stokel Waterfall > Benang Kelambu Waterfall > Seafood Dinner
days.description: Today is dedicated to Lombok's most spectacular waterfall trail. After breakfast, drive into the lush interior to reach Benang Stokel Waterfall — a dramatic two-tier cascade tumbling through ancient tropical forest. A short walk further reveals Benang Kelambu, described by visitors as one of Indonesia's most unique waterfalls, where multiple streams of water flow downward through hanging curtains of green vegetation, creating a natural living waterfall curtain. Enjoy lunch near the falls. In the afternoon, sample traditional Lombok snacks and browse for T-shirts and batik textiles at local shops before heading to a pearl shop — Lombok is one of the world's leading producers of high-quality freshwater and saltwater pearls. Wind up the day with a fresh seafood dinner by the beach, a classic Lombok experience.
suggestedActivities: Trek to Benang Stokel twin waterfall
Walk behind the curtain falls at Benang Kelambu
Fresh seafood dinner by the beach

days.title: Lombok Souvenirs > Banyumulek Village > Departure
days.description: A leisurely hotel breakfast and checkout before one final morning exploring Lombok's artisan heritage. Visit Banyumulek Pottery Village for the last time — a community where 80% of residents are native Sasak potters, hand-shaping beautiful terracotta pieces using techniques passed down over generations. Browse the workshops, watch the potters at work, and pick up a unique souvenir to take home. Your guide then transfers you to Lombok International Airport for your departure flight.
suggestedActivities: Watch master potters at work at Banyumulek

sections.heading: 4-Day Lombok Nature Tour from Malaysia — Waterfalls, Islands & Culture
sections.body: This 4-day Lombok package is built for travellers who want to go beyond the beach. Starting from RM988 per person, it covers the full range of what makes Lombok extraordinary: the island paradise of Gili Trawangan, the jungle waterfall at Sendang Gile, the twin cascades of Benang Stokel and Benang Kelambu, and the living Sasak cultural villages. Malaysian passport holders enter Indonesia visa-free for up to 30 days, and direct flights from Kuala Lumpur to Lombok take just under 3 hours. With all transport, meals, and entrance fees included in a private tour format, this is the most comprehensive way to experience Lombok without the hassle of planning.

sections.heading: Lombok vs Bali — Why Nature Lovers Choose Lombok
sections.body: Lombok receives a fraction of Bali's tourist numbers but offers many of the same attractions — beaches, temples, volcanos, and incredible snorkelling — plus a quieter, more authentic atmosphere. The Sasak culture is distinct from Bali's Hindu traditions, the waterfalls are less visited, and the Gili Islands retain a genuine island paradise feel. For Malaysians looking for a fresh Indonesian destination, Lombok in 4 days delivers extraordinary value and a genuinely different experience from Bali.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Lombok, Indonesia?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders can enter Indonesia visa-free for stays of up to 30 days. Ensure your passport is valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates.

items.question: How fit do I need to be for the waterfall walks?
items.answer: The walks to Sendang Gile, Benang Stokel, and Benang Kelambu involve moderate trails through forest — mostly flat or gently sloping with some uneven ground. Comfortable walking shoes or trainers are recommended. The walks are suitable for most fitness levels, including older adults and older children.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Lombok for waterfalls?
items.answer: The waterfalls are most impressive during or just after the wet season (November to March) when water flow is at its strongest. The dry season (May to September) is more reliable for beach activities and Gili Trawangan snorkelling. Both seasons offer rewarding experiences.

items.question: Are flights included in this package?
items.answer: No, flights are not included. We recommend flying from Kuala Lumpur (KUL) to Lombok International Airport (LOP). AirAsia operates direct flights regularly.

items.question: Can I do a shorter version of this tour?
items.answer: Yes — our 3-day Lombok Island package covers the cultural highlights and Gili Trawangan for just RM688 per person. The 4-day version adds the iconic waterfall experiences that most travellers find to be the highlight of their trip.
